sections.heading: What is AndroidManifest.xml?
sections.content: AndroidManifest.xml is a required configuration file in every Android app. It tells the Android system about the app’s components, permissions, hardware requirements, and entry point before the app runs.

sections.heading: What the Manifest Declares
items: All app components (Activities, Services, Receivers, Providers)

rows.description: Contains all app components and app-level settings
Handles system or app broadcast events

sections.heading: Important <application> Attributes
items: android:label – App name shown to users
android:theme – Default theme for the app
android:allowBackup – Allows backup to Google Drive

sections.heading: Intent Filters
sections.content: Intent filters define what type of intents a component can respond to. The launcher Activity must include MAIN action and LAUNCHER category so Android knows which screen to open when the user launches the app.

rows.purpose: Display data like URLs or files
Select data such as contacts or images

sections.heading: App Permissions
items: Dangerous permissions require runtime user approval

rows.description: Granted only to apps with same signing key

sections.heading: Common Manifest Mistakes
items: Forgetting to declare a new Activity
Not declaring INTERNET permission for network calls
Requesting dangerous permissions without runtime handling

sections.heading: Summary
sections.content: AndroidManifest.xml is the central configuration file of an Android app. It defines app components, permissions, and how the system interacts with your app. Proper configuration prevents crashes and ensures smooth app behavior.
